# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Rails mailer class that sends emails via SendGrid. Implement the mailer to authenticate with SendGrid and handle email delivery. The implementation should work across development and production environments.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

# app/mailers/application_mailer.rb
class ApplicationMailer < ActionMailer::Base
  default from: 'your_email@example.com'
  layout 'mailer'

  def sendgrid_settings
    if Rails.env.development?
      {
        api_key: ENV['SENDGRID_API_KEY_DEVELOPMENT'],
        host: 'smtp.sendgrid.net',
        port: 587,
        domain: 'example.com',
        authentication: :plain
      }
    elsif Rails.env.production?
      {
        api_key: ENV['SENDGRID_API_KEY_PRODUCTION'],
        host: 'smtp.sendgrid.net',
        port: 587,
        domain: 'example.com',
        authentication: :plain
      }
    end
  end

  def send_email(to, subject, body)
    mail(
      to: to,
      subject: subject,
      body: body
    ).deliver_now
  end
end

# config/environments/development.rb
Rails.application.configure do
  ActionMailer::Base.delivery_method = :smtp
  ActionMailer::Base.smtp_settings = ApplicationMailer.sendgrid_settings
end

# config/environments/production.rb
Rails.application.configure do
  ActionMailer::Base.delivery_method = :smtp
  ActionMailer::Base.smtp_settings = ApplicationMailer.sendgrid_settings
end

# Example usage in a controller or another service
class UserMailer < ApplicationMailer
  def welcome_email(user)
    @user = user
    mail(to: @user.email, subject: 'Welcome to Our Site')
  end
end
